# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7-slim-buster

LABEL maintainer="legacy-team@example.com" \
      description="Legacy Python 2.7 application - migration to Python 3 planned" \
      python.version="2.7"

RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get upgrade -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        build-essential \
        libssl-dev \
        libffi-dev \
        ca-certificates \
        curl \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* /tmp/* /var/tmp/*

RUN groupadd --gid 1001 appgroup && \
    useradd --uid 1001 --gid appgroup --shell /usr/sbin/nologin --create-home appuser

WORKDIR /app

COPY requirements.txt /app/requirements.txt

RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip==20.3.4 setuptools==44.1.1 && \
    pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup . /app/

RUN find /app -type f -name "*.py" -exec chmod 644 {} \; && \
    find /app -type d -exec chmod 755 {} \; && \
    chmod -R o-w /app

RUN test ! -f /app/.env || (echo "ERROR: .env file detected in build context. Remove secrets from image." && exit 1)
RUN test ! -d /app/.git || (echo "ERROR: .git directory detected in build context. Add to .dockerignore." && exit 1)

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    PYTHONHASHSEED=random \
    PIP_NO_CACHE_DIR=1 \
    LC_ALL=C.UTF-8 \
    LANG=C.UTF-8

RUN mkdir -p /app/logs /app/tmp && \
    chown -R appuser:appgroup /app/logs /app/tmp && \
    chmod 750 /app/logs /app/tmp

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1

EXPOSE 8080

USER appuser

CMD ["python", "app.py"]
